back_translation: Early in the morning Jerubbaal (that is, Gideon) and all the people with him camped beside the spring of Harod. The camp of Midian was to the north of them, by the hill of Moreh in the valley.
The LORD said to Gideon, 'The people with you are too many for Me to deliver Midian into their hands, lest Israel boast over Me, saying, My own hand has saved me.'
'Now therefore, proclaim in the ears of the people: Whoever is fearful and trembling, let him return and depart from Mount Gilead.' So 22,000 people returned, and 10,000 remained.

The LORD said to Gideon, 'The people are still too many. Bring them down to the water, and I will sift them for you there. Whoever I say to you, This one shall go with you — he shall go with you; and whoever I say to you, This one shall not go with you — he shall not go.'
Gideon brought the people down to the water, and the LORD said to him, 'Separate those who lap the water with their tongues as a dog laps, from those who kneel down to drink.'
The number of those who lapped the water with their hands to their mouths was 300 men; all the rest of the people knelt down on their knees to drink water.
The LORD said to Gideon, 'By the 300 men who lapped I will save you and deliver the Midianites into your hand. Let all the rest of the people go, each to his place.'

So the 300 took the provisions of the people in their hands and their rams' horns. Gideon sent all the rest of the men of Israel each to his tent but kept the 300 men. The camp of Midian was below him in the valley.
That night the LORD said to him, 'Arise, go down against the camp, for I have delivered it into your hand.'
'But if you are afraid to go down, go down to the camp with Purah your servant,'

'and listen to what they say; afterward your hands will be strengthened, and you will go down against the camp.' So he went down with Purah his servant to the outskirts of the armed men who were in the camp.
The Midianites, the Amalekites, and all the people of the east had settled in the valley like a swarm of locusts, and their camels were innumerable, like the sand on the shore of the sea.

When Gideon arrived, a man was telling his friend a dream. 'Behold, I dreamed a dream,' he said, 'and behold, a loaf of barley bread was tumbling into the camp of Midian and came to the tent and struck it. It fell, turned upside down, and the tent fell flat.'
His friend answered and said, 'This is nothing other than the sword of Gideon son of Joash, the Israelite. God has delivered Midian and the whole camp into his hand.'
When Gideon heard the dream and its interpretation, he bowed in worship. He returned to the camp of Israel and said, 'Arise, for the LORD has delivered the camp of Midian into your hands.'
He divided the 300 men into three companies, gave each man a ram's horn in his hand and an empty jar with a torch inside the jar.
He said to them, 'Watch me and do likewise. Behold, when I come to the outskirts of the camp, do exactly as I do.'
'When I and all who are with me blow the horn, then you too blow your horns all around the camp, and shout, A sword for the LORD and for Gideon!'

Gideon and the 100 men with him came to the outskirts of the camp at the beginning of the middle watch, just after they had set the guard, and they blew the horns and shattered the jars in their hands.
The three companies blew the horns and broke the jars, holding the torches in their left hands and the horns in their right hands to blow, and they cried, 'A sword for the LORD and for Gideon!'
Each man stood in his place around the camp, and the entire camp ran, crying out as they fled.

When the 300 horns sounded, the LORD set every man's sword against his fellow throughout the camp, and the camp fled as far as Beth-shittah, toward Zererah, as far as the border of Abel-meholah near Tabbath.
Then the men of Israel were called out from Naphtali, from Asher, and from all Manasseh, and they pursued the Midianites.

Gideon sent messengers throughout the hill country of Ephraim, saying, 'Come down against the Midianites and seize the waters before them, as far as Beth-barah and the Jordan.' So all the men of Ephraim were called out, and they seized the waters as far as Beth-barah and the Jordan.

They also captured two princes of Midian — Oreb and Zeeb. They killed Oreb at the rock of Oreb, and they killed Zeeb at the winepress of Zeeb. They pursued the Midianites further, and brought the heads of Oreb and Zeeb to Gideon beyond the Jordan.
